lesson.menu_title: Conference Calls with HQ -- Giving Updates
lesson.menu_desc: Report what people said on a conference call with headquarters using reported speech: Amy said the order was on track, they told me the deadline would be Friday -- said/told + backshift (is to was, will to would, can to could) -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 16 -- Conference Calls with HQ: Giving Updates
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Roberto Rezende | Aula 16 -- Conference Calls with HQ: Giving Updates

listenings.text: Hi Roberto, this is Amy. I am calling to brief you before the conference call with headquarters tomorrow. Here is the latest. The team in Shanghai will dial in at nine, our time. They are two hours ahead, so for them it is the afternoon. The call is about the big order. There is good news and one concern. The good news: the order is on track and the engines are ready. The concern: the client wants a small change, and that part is behind schedule. So when HQ asks for the status, here is what to do. Give a clear update. Then report what the client told you. For example, say: the client said they needed a small change. They told me they wanted it next week. Remember to use reported speech. When you report what someone said, the verbs move back: is becomes was, will becomes would, can becomes could. Do not just read your notes. Tell the story of what each person said. Keep it short, keep it clear, and speak on behalf of the whole team. Good luck, and let me know how the call goes.

This is a quick summary of the conference call with headquarters this morning. I dialed in at nine. The team in Shanghai joined a few minutes later. First, I gave the status of the big order. I said the engines were ready and the order was on track. Then headquarters asked about the small change the client wanted. I reported what the client had told me. I said the client wanted one part redesigned, and they told me they needed it the following week. HQ said that was fine. They told me the new part would be ready in time. We agreed on the next steps, and I spoke on behalf of the whole sales team. The latest news is good: everyone is aligned, and the project is back on track. I will send the full report to Amy this afternoon.

extra_audio.text: First, dial in to the conference call a few minutes early. Then, greet headquarters and the team. After that, give a clear status update on your project. Next, report what the client said using reported speech. Finally, summarize the next steps on behalf of the team and end the call.
